gift_format_validity.note: $CATEGORY header present; all 5 blocks parse as ::id:: stem { = correct ~ ~ ~ } with #### general feedback; no structural errors found.

positional_bias.note: Derived positions [2,0,1,3,2] match sidecar [2,0,1,3,2] exactly — no meta_gift_desync. max_share == 0.40 is not > 0.40; boundary is a pass.

length_bias.note: No question's correct answer exceeded mean_distractor * 1.20. Previous failure (rate 0.80) is fully resolved — builder lengthened distractors and/or trimmed correct answers across all 5 questions.

weakest_questions.distractor: Because circuits can only recognise visual shapes, not abstract symbols
weakest_questions.reason: Circuits have no visual-shape perception — the claim is physically incoherent in a way beginners are unlikely to hold as a genuine misconception. Flagged soft; does not breach 0.25 threshold.

distractor_plausibility.note: 1 of 15 distractors scored 1 (absurd). Rate 0.067 is well under the 0.25 threshold. All other 14 distractors scored 3-4 as plausible beginner misconceptions.

coverage_map.q1: Explain, in plain language, why computers can only process numbers — not raw text, images, or sound.
coverage_map.q2: Describe what it means for real-world information to be converted into numbers.
coverage_map.q3: Give at least two everyday examples of data (text, image, audio) and explain how each becomes a set of numbers before an AI can use it.
coverage_map.q4: Identify why a shared number-based language makes it possible for a single AI system to handle many different types of information.
coverage_map.q5: Recognise that mathematical operations on those numbers are what give AI its ability to find patterns, make predictions, and generate outputs.

scope_discipline.note: All questions stay within corpus §5.1-5.4. Parameters/weights in q5 are introduced in corpus §5.4 and are in-scope. No vectors, embeddings, dot-product, or cosine anywhere in stems, correct answers, or distractors.

verdict: Pass — all 5 checks clean. Length bias fully resolved (rate 0.0, down from 0.80 in prior run). Positional bias at boundary (0.40, not > 0.40). One weak distractor in q1 scored absurd but absurd_rate 0.067 is well under 0.25 threshold. All 5 LOs covered one-to-one with no orphans. No out-of-scope content, no forbidden terms.
